per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA describes how embedded content behaves, while the REG requires that electronically presented icons be machine-readable; they do not express the same or directly conflicting constraint.

The REA concerns embedded content behavior on other websites, while the REG states an exception where Article 9(1) does not apply to personal data manifestly made public by the data subject; no directly comparable constraint is stated.
The REA describes embedded content behavior, whereas the REG provides that Paragraph 1 shall not apply if specified conditions apply; there is no quote-supported overlap or conflict.
